Module 6: The Sales Sequence

Questions to Explore

From Direct Response 2.0 Workbook

Parallel Realities/Divergent Roads

Additional Questions:

Sales Themeplates

EXAMPLE:

Sales Sequence Checklist:





Questions to Explore

From Direct Response 2.0 Workbook

Parallel Realities/Divergent Roads 



In vivid detail, what does “success” look like in 3 days from day?



3 weeks?



3 months?



12 months



In vivid detail, what would their life look like 3 days from now if they DON’T buy your product?



3 weeks?



3 months?



12 months? 



Additional Questions: 



What beliefs/micro-commitments have they agreed to that you can call on them to stay consistent with? 



What are the questions a PERFECT buyer would ask (or have asked) before saying “yes”? 



What case studies/stories/proof elements can you link those beliefs and reframes to? (how can you place those beliefs in the context of what your offer will deliver)?



What Mid-Cart Bonus can you offer for 24 hours that would either:

Set them up for the “next thing” AFTER your course

Help them achieve an exciting outcome as presented in your case study



What’s the real, hyper-specific cost of going backwards (Risk Revealer)? 



What’s the big benefit of moving forward (future pace the results/outcomes) 



What are the 3 biggest initial objections that eventual buyers have? How can you frame that skepticism as a positive trait (or the trait of someone who eventually invests)? 



Sales Themeplates

Themeplate 11: Upgraded FAQ



About: 



The FAQ is a staple in just about every launch campaign.



Traditionally used to counter objections, the FAQ is actually capable of doing so much more. 



When done right, your UPGRADED FAQ has the power to counter objections, position you against competitors, reinforce the big “ahas” you laid out in the pre-launch, create safety by pre-paving the outcome, help them identify as a buyer… all without breaking form.



I like to use this email about 48 hours before close… in other words, the final email in the sales sequence, before rolling into the close. 



General Format:



The lead-in which frames questions or even skepticism as positive traits IN-LINE with being a buyer. 



The FAQ

Q1: Objection 1: Take their biggest objection - and transform it into their biggest opportunity. 

Q2: Objection 2: Take objection 2 and use it to point to a SPECIFIC and unique aspect of your product. 

Q3: Objection 3: Bust a major industry-wide myth (sacred cow) that in turn, empowers them to take action.

Q4: Position against competitors and get BUY-IN from them on your unique way of doing things. 

Q5: Use it as a spring-board into your fuller USP.

Q6: Answer the “time” objection or some other universal one. 

Q7: Use it to springboard into the offer while keeping frame.

Q8: Use it to for risk-reversal (and guarantees) without breaking frame. 



THEMEPLATE



[Intro describing your reader but making it sound like you're simply describing your best prospect] 

[Validation of your reader - you appreciate that type of person]

[Why you appreciate that person, connected to the product you're selling]

[Tell him what you're about to tell him]

[Assure him he'll find answers to his top 2 questions] 

[Question]

[Answer] 

[Repeat for as many FAQs as you've got, being specific and tonal where and as necessary]



[Organize questions almost like a salespage, opening with more of their problem-related questions and finishing the list with closer questions, like what exactly they'll receive,what happens immediately after ordering and risk-reducers]



[Light sign-off]

[Signature]



PS: [Scarcity, urgency and other FOMOs]



{CTA}



EXAMPLE:



Subject: 

Questions people asked before saying YES to Courses That Convert...



Body: 



The business owners who get the MOST out of my programs are usually the ones who ask the most questions.



The ones who refuse to let me off the hook.



The ones who hold my feet to the fire and dare to ask the hard-nosed questions.



Personally, I love it.



Because at the end of the day, I only want you to join Courses That Convert if you know, without a doubt, that it’s the missing link between you and a fully-leveraged and profitable online business.



So without further ado, here are the most common questions I’ve been fielding over the last few days.



If you’re still on the fence, chances are at least one or two of them has been wreaking havoc on your sleep patterns.



(sorry about that).



Let’s jump in!





1. What if there are other courses on my topic?



Awesome! That means there’s a market…



… and an opportunity ;-)



Because where there’s competition, there are also dissatisfied customers who DIDN’T get their problem solved the first time.



Your job is to discover how current courses have let them down, note the failure points, and create a course that rescues them from those issues and helps them achieve the results they’re looking for.





2. What if I don’t have a list?



Sure, it would be awesome to go into Courses That Convert with an active list. But your success certainly won’t depend on it.  



There’s a crazy myth that you need to have a list of subscribers that trust you enough to tell you what they want, but in reality that’s just one of MANY course validation strategies.



In Module 1 of Courses That Convert, you’ll learn how to gauge market interest and create a course that your market wants EVEN if you don’t have an email list to send surveys to (yet). 



I call it the minimum viable validation (MVV) method, and it’s the perfect way to get up and running fast! No list needed.





3. How long do I need to be creating free content (blogs, podcasts, etc.) BEFORE I create a course?



I bet you’ve heard something along the lines of “you need to give free valuable content for 6, 9 or 12 months before you’ve earned the right to create a course."



Ugh.



Where does this advice even come from?



As long as there’s a PROBLEM your target audience experiences that you’re uniquely equipped to solve, it doesn’t matter if you’ve been offering free content for 10 years or 10 days.



Yes, being in the game longer may give you more insights into what the market wants...  but these aspects can easily be overcome with the strategies you’ll learn inside Courses That Convert.



In short. You don’t need permission. You need proper strategy and implementation.



4. I’ve seen other course building programs that also go deep into the marketing side. Does Courses That Convert do that?  



Conversion is a two-sided process.



Yes, marketing funnels and promotional strategies are sexy.



But as I’m sure you’ve experienced, they also get overwhelming and lead you down an endless rabbit hole of delay and distraction.



I want to remind you that what you’re doing inside Courses That Convert is building a REAL profitable asset that can generate income for the rest of your life!



It’s a process that requires laser-focus, meticulous step-by-step detail, and a disciplined approach that back-burns all tactics that don’t serve the immediate goal of building your course.



I know this will turn off the shiny object seekers. But that’s okay.



My obligation isn’t to them… it’s to YOU!



The driven, focused entrepreneur who’s made the big decision to BUILD a valuable long term asset with as little distraction as possible.



And if you do have a slight case of funnel fever (don’t we all), you’ll get access to a very special bonus: 



“The Course Launch Master Plan” that reveals the EXACT campaign I use in my own 7-figure course launches, and more importantly, the downloadable step-by-step project plan that allows you to replicate it! 





5. Will I really have time to build my own online course? In short, the busier you are, the MORE you need a leveraged, freedom-building, profit generating online course in your business.



And I get it.



You’re a super busy entrepreneur with a million things (and maybe even a day job) on your plate. That’s why Courses That Convert was built with one thing in mind:



Implementation.



We’ve laid out all the steps in crystal clear fashion so that you don’t have to worry about being your own “project manager”.



Instead, you just have to block off a couple hours a week, dive in and follow the step-by-step processes that guides you towards your first profitable online course.



6. How many hours will I need to invest every week?



Courses That Convert is self-paced.



Once you enroll, you’ll have instant access to all 5 Modules (plus the Tech Library) so that you can build your course at whatever speed your situation allows.



I’d say that at a minimum, you should be ready to invest at least 2-3 hours per week. A small time investment considering you’re building an asset that could be worth more than most people’s homes.



7. Can you remind me what I’m getting when I enroll TODAY:



Of course :)



When you enroll today during this special PROMOTIONAL PERIOD, you’ll get access to:



The FULL Courses That Convert Implementation Program with access to all 5 video modules, cheat sheets, resource guides, project plans, and every tool you need to build your first profitable course with confidence and ease.

 

You will all get access to my entire Tech Library with 10+ step-by-step videos to ensure you know exactly how to implement every step of the way.



Bonus #1 : Access to the Courses That Convert Facebook Group where you’ll interact with like-minded course creators, form valuable partnerships. trade feedback, and receive virtual high-fives and encouragement every step of the way.



Bonus #2: The Course Launch Master Plan that gives you a COMPLETE step-by-step action plan for launching your course in a powerful and profitable way without the overwhelm.  Value - $497



Bonus #3: How to Create A High-Converting Sales Page so that you can confidently create a winning sales page that maximizes conversions and gets your course into the hands of those who need it most. Value - $297



8. Is it really risk free?



Yep! I don’t want you to foot the bill unless Courses That Convert really is the missing link between you and owning a profitable online course.



That’s why I’m giving you a FULL 60 DAYS to put the plan into action before making a final commitment. If you do the work (details here) and decide this course is not right for you, just let us know and we will refund your investment. 



So that’s it, Ryan.



At this point you should have everything you need to make the best choice for you and your business.



If that road leads you into Courses That Convert….



Then I look forward to meeting you on the inside.



ALL my best,



Amy



P.S. If you DO decide to join me and hundreds of your peers inside Courses That Convert, then make sure to do it before the special promo period expires.



I’d hate for you to lose access to the the high impact bonuses.



<Call to Action Button>





Themeplate 12: The Curious Case (w/ Mid-Cart Bonus) 



About: 



We use this themeplate to back up our product’s biggest claim with a compelling (and curiosity-arousing) case study -- and then to introduce a mid-cart bonus that further supports and syncs up with the outcomes produced in the case study. 



THEMEPLATE



[Re-Affirm the big sales argument around your launch] 

[Acknowledge that they haven’t fully bought in]

[Briefly future pace the immediate ownership experience]

[CTA 1 - Introduce a Case Study without revealing too much detail]

[Build desire around those outcomes] 

[CTA 2]

[PS: Add the mid-cart bonus related to the case study] 





EXAMPLE: 



Subject:

[Bonus Added] An extra 160K from this sneaky little sequence? 



Body:



Don’t know about you…



But I’m getting kinda tired of ramblin’on about how email copy is the fastest and most powerful way to double, triple or even 10X the sales from your sales funnel. 



Blah… blah… blah..



Do I believe it?



Hell ya. 



I’ve had a front-row seat to those “miracles” for over half a decade strong. 



But more importantly… 



Do you believe it?



Probably not.



I mean, we’ve been talking about the power of email for just over a week now…



So even if you “kinda”, “sorta” sensed it to be true -- if you REALLY believed it, you’d already be inside 10X Launches blazing through the videos, downloading one of the 20+ proven themeplates, and firing off profit bomb after profit bomb as quickly as your over-caffeinated fingers and ConvertKit account could handle.



But you ain’t.



You’re still here. 



So clearly we both have some work to do ;-)



But instead of trying to hammer you with more ideas around why you’re totally missing out on the email marketing game… 



… and how in literally the SAME amount of time you’ve spent reading that 1,800 word blog post about the latest rule changes Zucks’n’co rolled out to eff up your facebook stgy… 



You could’ve quickly knocked out a sneaky 4-part email sequence that brought in MORE extra revenue than Zucks will ever reward you with.



But I’m done with conjecture and arm-twisting. 



Time to “lawyer up” and lay out the evidence.  



And to appease that rational part of your brain that keeps asking “is 10X Launches really worth it?”...



We put together this short case study for you here to answer that for yourself; 



Presenting Exhibit A:  



$164K of extra profit from just 4 short emails (WATCH THE CASE STUDY)



To put that into perspective…



That’s enough lift in profit to cover the salaries of 3 FULL-TIME employees for an entire year. 



… One to learn about the latest facebook changes so that you don’t have to. 

 

… Another to deal with all the techy funnel stuff while you crank out another winning sales email from 10X Launches to plug into it, 



… And a THIRD to sit around and listen to you rave about how you finally “get” this whole  email copywriting thing… but only wish you jumped in sooner. 



Trust me… you’ll want to keep the third employee around. 



The excitement around actually EXECUTING never wears off ;- )



Give the $164K Case Study a spin…



… and when you’re ready to start filling in those little email icon things on your funnel map with the kidna words that inundate your iPhone with Paypal notifications… 



Meet us inside 10X Launches



(sign off) 



PS. 



You didn’t really think we were gonna hype you up with such a “holy crap I can’t wait to do this in my own biz” case study and NOT show you how to plug in the same 4 emails into your own funnel did you?



Of course you didn’t ;-) 



Enroll by TONIGHT and we’ll pre-load your 10X Launches account with an additional BONUS that will walk you through how to execute those same emails in your own campaign.



=> K, you’ve made your case. Add this BONUS to my account so I can start 10X’ing already



Themeplate 13 - The Point of No Return



About: 



Highlights the cost of going backwards by detailing the “burning crash” in the rearview. Painting a picture that the prospect will want to drive as far away from as possible. 



Intimacy with stakes/risk-revealer: create more tension in going backwards than moving forwards. 



THEMEPLATE: 

[List out what “Tomorrow” will hold for them independent of your product]

[Future pace what success using your product can look like] 

[Show them the burning crash in the “Rearview” - MOHT]

[Re-state the major reframes/sacred cows from the pre-launch] 

[Invite them to move forward with your program] 

[CTA]  



EXAMPLE:



Subject:: 

Burning Man vs. Broken Funnels



Body: 



The doors to 10X Launches are now open.



But whether or not you join… 



… you’re still going to be building funnels. 



… you’re still going to be launching new online courses, masterminds and programs. 



…. you’re still going to be manning the controls of your ConvertKit, MailChimp, Drip, Infusionsoft or wtv-the-heck you’re using to send out the messages that get people to buy. 



And the growth of your online business, the number of employees you can bring in to do the stuff you’re not great at, and the number of weeks you can take off this year to do the things you SWORE you’d finally cross off your bucket list… like that trip to Bali, hiking machu picchu, or doing whatever the hell people do at Burning Man…



WIll all be a result of what happens in the moments after you hit *send*.



No pressure ;-) 



But here’s where you have a choice.



We can pull a good-old fashioned “Men-in-Black” memory swipe on you, and erase everything you’ve learnt over the last 7 days.



That way, you can confidently dig your head in the proverbial sand and return to the cozy and familiar narrative that… 



…. “all I need is latest funnel. Who cares about the pesky execution thing? Psh” 



… “even if I need to execute… I’ll just throw a bone to one of those copywriters clamoring for work. They’re all super cheap and available.”  



… “uh oh… guess I was wrong. It’s cool. I got this. I’ll put some bulletproof coffee on the pot, swipe a bit of this -- a bit of that, and fire it off. And because I have this super fancy funnel and some ninja facebook retargeting stuff rolling out, the copy thing can’t be THAT important”



… oh sh*t…. why isn’t this converting? My facebook ads must be broken! My pixel must not be firing correctly. Wait… I don’t even know what a pixel is, but i heard it was important. Or maybe I should’ve done a webinar! Ahhh. All good. I’m gonna fail forward. I’ll buy that new program or funnel course and *CRUSH IT* next time. 



Whew. Painful.



Glad we’re graduating out of that ;-) 



Here’s your second option: 



You can leverage the hard-earned insights you’ve integrated into your synapses over this  past week.



… that EXECUTION is everything. And that the “funnel” will only be as strong as the words that fill it.



… that you, and you alone, are responsible for executing. Sure, there’s a time and place to bring on a copywriter… but just like some of the world’s top entrepreneurs still write their own stuff, it’s your job, as someone who’s in charge of bringing in profits, to take full ownership and control.



… and that, hey… it’s not as crazy hard as it looks. “And WOW I don’t even need to memorize formulas, shoehorn my words into rigid templates, or handwrite old sales letters until my wrist hurts before being confident enough to knock out high-converting copy that makes the cash register ring…



… I can just get my 10X Launches login info, achieve minimum viable mastery over this launch copy thing… and use one of the 20+ proven themeplates to fuel my highest conversions yet”.



Yep. That’s an option.



And arguably the only one that will have you hiking your version of machu picchu by quarter’s end to celebrate your best launch yet. 



Cause whether or not you join 10X Launches…



… you’re in the online business game for the long haul.



Time to play it with a stacked deck 



(sign off)



Themeplate 14 - The Blind Eye 



About: 



Similar to the “Point of No Return”, the Blind Eye is used to FULLY release your prospect from past failures, and then remind them of the responsibility to act on the BETTER alternative you’ve shown them.



It coddles, and then it challenges/confronts, acting as a risk revealer and putting them in a privileged place to do something about it. 



THEMEPLATE



[detail the struggle - MOHT]

[release them from blame being VERY specific in why it wasn’t their fault]

[remind them of the non-serving beliefs you set up in re-launch] 

[hold them accountable to the new version - “not your fault until it is”] 

[future pace the new outcomes + how your program facilitates it]

[remind them of the commitments they made earlier]

[close the consistency loop] 



EXAMPLE: 





Subject: 

Real talk (and a VIP invitation) 



Body:



Almost every other day I get a teary-eyed facebook message from a yoga teacher kicking herself for spending YEARS barely scraping by… 



… doing everything in her power to make it a “go” despite the silent doubts from friends and family. 



… hustling around town to teach classes at any studio that will have her. 



… scrolling through the glossy instagram quote cards from her favorite yoga crushes wondering what the heck must be wrong with her? Why, after all these years, am I still stuck riding the bus to get to a gig I don’t even want to teach? 



Ouch. 



Let’s stop the self-blame for a sec ;-) 



Cause here’s the thing… 



It wasn’t your fault that the your YTT didn’t prepare you for the business world. 



… that it had you believing there was only 1 revenue stream available to you (and it required negotiating with stingy studio owners to get a piece of an ever-shrinking pie)



… or that it led you to the conclusion that all it takes is a few crystals, some mantras, and the right sequencing between them to magically land a slew of high paying private clients that allow you make teaching a full time thing.



Nope.



Can’t be blamed for just playing out the script you were handed. 



But if the current definition of being a “yoga teacher” isn’t serving you (and hasn’t been for awhile)…



You ARE responsible for graduating into a new definition.



One that empowers.



One that enriches.



One that takes your lofty intentions…



… and grounds them in the fertile soil of PROVEN online marketing strategies that have fueled the rapid rise of all your favorite yoga crushes.



You ARE responsible for learning about the 9 revenue streams available to you (4 of which are fully automated and passive)...



… and you ARE responsible for choosing the ones that sync up best with your authentic expression (we’ll help with that in Module 3)... and making use of the 6 group coaching calls to keep you on track, as for the FIRST time since you began this yoga journey, you truly feel a sense of…



“Wow. I got this” 



In short - you’re responsible for your own awesomeness…



And how you share it with others in a sustainable way that serves all. 



Over the last few days, you’ve steeped in this new definition.



You’ve sketched out some VERY REAL projections for your business…



… and met the new version of you capable of carrying them out.



Here’s your invitation to flow forward and leave the hustle behind.



(Sign off)

Sales Sequence Checklist:

Have you coaches your prospect on:



Proof that your offer aligns with the reframes/beliefs from the pre launch?

Case studies or stories that bolster + contextualize the sales argument 

Cost of moving backwards

Re-commitment to the journey they’re already on (closing the consistency loop)
